message:
build(profile): disable symbol stripping in release-perf profile

Flip `strip = true` → `strip = false` on the `release-perf` profile
(inherits from `release`, opt-level=3, debug=false).

Rationale: release-perf is the profile used for perf/benchmark builds
and CI reliability harnesses. Stripping symbols removes the
information `perf` / `cargo-flamegraph` / `addr2line` / asan backtraces
need to attribute time or faults to a specific function. The resulting
flamegraphs showed raw addresses and `??::<unknown>` frames instead of
the frankensqlite call stack, which defeated the point of a dedicated
perf profile.

Binary size grows by ~30-40% on this profile as a result; acceptable
since release-perf artifacts aren't shipped to users — they're local
and CI-only. The default `release` profile is unchanged, so production
binaries still strip normally.

`.beads/issues.jsonl` bumps by one status line from br sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -186,7 +186,7 @@ debug = true
 inherits = "release"
 opt-level = 3
 debug = false
-strip = true
+strip = false
 
 [profile.dev]
 opt-level = 1
